title: How Faith and Financial Freedom Create Purpose-Driven Leadership
guestBio: Spring Eatmon is an entrepreneur, faith leader, and legacy architect who retired from her executive career at Kaiser Permanente at age 28 to walk in purpose over paycheck. She co-leads Max Life Agency and The Legacy Lab with her husband Curtis, helping families and businesses build generational wealth and lasting legacy.
excerpt: Spring Eatmon retired herself at 28, choosing purpose over paycheck and building a legacy-focused business empire. Her conversation with Aimee reveals how faith, financial alignment, and fearless leadership create true freedom.
metaDescription: Discover how faith and financial freedom fuel purpose-driven leadership with Spring Eatmon on The Manage Her podcast.
keyTakeaways: Financial freedom isn't about the amount of money you have, but about your relationship with money and alignment with purpose.
Walking in purpose over paycheck requires courage, but creates authentic prosperity and fulfillment.
Partnership in marriage and business amplifies impact when both people are aligned with their calling.
Your wealth begins with understanding your worth, not the other way around.
Building generational wealth requires intentional planning and legacy-focused thinking.
Women must rewrite their relationship with money to step into their full leadership potential.
Faith-based decision making can lead to greater financial and personal freedom than traditional career paths.

pullQuotes.text: Your wealth begins with your worth and your legacy begins today

content:
What would it take for you to walk away from a successful executive career at 28 years old? For Spring Eatmon, it wasn't about having all the answers or even a foolproof plan. It was about choosing alignment over comfort, purpose over paycheck, and faith over fear.

In this powerful conversation on The Manage Her, Spring shares her journey from Kaiser Permanente executive to co-leader of Max Life Agency and The Legacy Lab alongside her husband Curtis. Their story isn't just about building business success—it's about redefining what wealth, freedom, and leadership actually mean when you're operating from a place of deep faith and purpose.

Redefining Financial Freedom

Spring's perspective on financial freedom challenges everything we've been taught about money and success. True financial freedom isn't about having a certain dollar amount in your bank account—it's about your relationship with money and your alignment with purpose. When you understand that your wealth begins with your worth, not the other way around, everything changes.

This shift in thinking is particularly crucial for women leaders who often struggle with imposter syndrome or undervalue their contributions. Spring's journey demonstrates that when you operate from a place of knowing your worth, making faith-based decisions, and staying aligned with your calling, financial prosperity follows naturally. It's not about chasing money—it's about creating value and serving others in a way that honors both your gifts and your values.

The freedom that comes from this alignment cannot be overstated. When you're no longer driven by scarcity or the need to prove yourself through external markers of success, you can make decisions from a place of abundance and authenticity. This is where true leadership begins.

The Power of Purpose-Driven Partnership

One of the most compelling aspects of Spring's story is how she and her husband Curtis have built their business and legacy together. Their partnership demonstrates that when two people are aligned in purpose and committed to each other's growth, their impact multiplies exponentially.

This kind of partnership requires intentional communication, shared vision, and the willingness to support each other's individual calling within the context of your shared mission. It's not about losing your individual identity—it's about amplifying your impact through unity and complementary strengths.

For women leaders, this conversation offers permission to seek and create partnerships that honor both your ambition and your values. Whether in marriage, business, or friendship, the right partnerships can accelerate your growth and expand your influence in ways you never imagined possible.

Building Generational Wealth and Legacy

Through The Legacy Lab, Spring and Curtis are helping families and businesses think beyond immediate success to generational impact. This requires a fundamental shift from short-term thinking to legacy-focused planning. It's about making decisions today that will benefit not just your immediate family, but generations to come.

Building generational wealth isn't just about accumulating assets—it's about creating systems, values, and opportunities that can be passed down. This includes financial literacy, entrepreneurial thinking, strong family values, and a commitment to serving others. It's wealth in the fullest sense of the word.

For women leaders, this perspective offers a different framework for success. Instead of constantly climbing the next rung of someone else's ladder, you can focus on building something that reflects your values and creates lasting impact. This shift alone can transform how you approach every business decision, investment, and career move.

Rewriting Your Relationship with Money

Perhaps one of the most important conversations Spring brings to the table is about women and money. Too many successful women still carry limiting beliefs about money that prevent them from stepping into their full potential. Whether it's undercharging for services, avoiding financial conversations, or feeling guilty about wealth, these patterns hold us back from the impact we're meant to make.

Spring's journey demonstrates what becomes possible when you heal your relationship with money and understand that financial success is not just acceptable—it's necessary for the level of impact you want to create. Money is a tool for good when it's in the hands of purpose-driven leaders who understand their responsibility to use it wisely.

This means getting comfortable with charging what you're worth, having difficult financial conversations, making strategic investments, and yes, accumulating wealth. Not for the sake of wealth itself, but for the freedom and impact it creates when managed with wisdom and purpose.

Leading with Faith and Conviction

Throughout her story, Spring demonstrates what it looks like to lead with both faith and business acumen. This isn't about choosing between spiritual values and financial success—it's about integrating them in a way that creates authentic prosperity.

Faith-based leadership requires courage because you're often making decisions that don't make sense to everyone around you. It requires conviction because you're operating from principles that go beyond immediate gratification or popular opinion. And it requires wisdom because you're steward of resources and influence that extend beyond yourself.

For women leaders, Spring's example offers permission to bring your whole self to your leadership—including your values, your faith, and your vision for legacy. You don't have to compartmentalize these aspects of yourself to be successful. In fact, the integration of faith and business often creates the most sustainable and fulfilling success.

Spring Eatmon's story is ultimately about courage—the courage to choose purpose over paycheck, alignment over approval, and legacy over immediate gratification. Her journey reminds us that our wealth truly does begin with our worth, and our legacy begins today with every decision we make.

quiz.title: What's Your Wealth & Purpose Alignment Style?
quiz.description: Discover how you approach the intersection of financial freedom, faith, and purposeful leadership. This quiz explores your unique relationship with money, meaning, and making impact.

questions.question: When you think about your current career path, what resonates most with you?

options.text: I feel called to something bigger, even if I can't see the whole staircase yet
I want to build something meaningful, but I need a solid plan first
I'm most excited about work that creates impact beyond just my own success
I'm questioning whether my current definition of success is actually mine

questions.question: How do you typically approach major life decisions?

options.text: I trust my gut and take action when something feels aligned, even without guarantees
I research thoroughly, create backup plans, and move when I feel prepared
I consider how my choices will affect the people I care about and our shared goals
I'm learning to listen to my inner voice instead of just external expectations

questions.question: What's your relationship with money like right now?

options.text: I'm learning that financial freedom is more about alignment than amount
I believe in building wealth systematically and being smart with every dollar
Money is a tool for creating impact and taking care of the people I love
I'm actively working to separate my worth from my net worth

questions.question: When you imagine your ideal work situation, what stands out?

options.text: Working with purpose every day, even if the income varies
Building something sustainable that grows steadily over time
Creating success alongside people who share my values and vision
Finally feeling like my work reflects who I really am

questions.question: How do you handle uncertainty in your professional life?

options.text: I've learned to see uncertainty as space for faith and growth to work
I create systems and strategies to minimize risks and maximize stability
I focus on how we can navigate challenges together rather than alone
I'm getting more comfortable with not having all the answers right now

questions.question: What kind of legacy do you want to create?

options.text: A story of courage and faith that inspires others to trust their calling
Generational wealth and wisdom that provides security for years to come
A ripple effect of positive impact through partnership and collaboration
An example of what's possible when you choose authenticity over approval

results.title: The Faith-Forward Leaper
results.description: You're someone who trusts your inner compass even when the path isn't perfectly clear. You understand that purpose often requires stepping away from traditional security, and you're willing to make bold moves when they align with your values. Your relationship with money is evolving as you learn to trust that doing meaningful work will create the abundance you need. Spring's journey from executive to entrepreneur will deeply resonate with your spirit of faithful courage.

results.title: The Strategic Builder
results.description: You believe in creating solid foundations before making big moves, and there's wisdom in your approach. You're naturally gifted at seeing the long-term picture and building wealth systematically. While you may sometimes overthink opportunities, your methodical nature creates lasting security. You value both purpose and practicality, seeking ways to align them thoughtfully. Spring's insights on redefining financial freedom and building generational wealth will give you fresh perspectives on balancing security with purpose.

results.title: The Collaborative Visionary
results.description: You understand that the best success comes from lifting others while you rise. Partnership and shared impact energize you more than individual achievement ever could. You're naturally drawn to creating legacy and meaning through your work, though you may sometimes undervalue your own contributions. Your heart for collaboration is a leadership superpower. Spring's story about building alongside her husband and redefining wealth through service will inspire your next level of impact.

results.title: The Awakening Leader
results.description: You're in a season of questioning what success really means and feeling called to something deeper than your current path. There's a stirring in you that knows your worth isn't tied to your paycheck, even if you're still figuring out what that looks like practically. You're ready to rewrite your relationship with money and leadership. Spring's transformation and insights about choosing alignment over comfort will provide the roadmap you've been seeking for your own purposeful pivot.

transcript: [music] Welcome back to the manage her where we explore the art of leading, building, and thriving as women who manage businesses, families, and everything in between. Today's guest is someone who embodies purpose and prosperity with deep spiritual grounding. Spring Eatman is a powerhouse entrepreneur, wife, mother of four, and faith leader who retired herself from her executive role at Kaiser Permanente at just 28 years old. She and her husband Curtis now co-lead Max Life Agency and the Legacy Lab, helping families and companies build generational wealth through alignment and purpose. She's spoken on stages alongside icons like Kobe Bryant, Les Brown, and Fawn Weaver. But what I love most about her is how authentically she leads with faith, family, and legacy at the center of it all. In this conversation, we're diving into how women can reclaim their relationship with money, why financial empowerment begins with spiritual alignment, and how legacy isn't something you leave, it's something you live. Let's welcome the incredible Spring Eatman to the manager. Thank you so much for being here, Spring. Hello. I'm so excited to be here. This is amazing. Thank you. Oh my gosh. Well, since I saw you on stage at the Elevate Conference, I was like, I have to have Spring on the show, and I'm so so grateful that you're on here today. Thank you so much. You have a really important message for all of us women out there, and >> I'm super glad to share that with everybody and get your word out there. So, let's start with your journey. You made the bold move to retire yourself from your corporate role at 28 and that is incredible. What shifted you that made you take that leap? >> Basically, you know, I was working there for about 5 years at the time. I had worked my way up the corporate ladder. So, I started as a on call part-time float receptionist. So, I like barely had a job there. Like someone legitimately had a call off of work for me to have a job that day. And [laughter] um but I was somebody that I came from really humble beginnings. My mom worked four or five jobs like most of my life. So she always taught me that don't be afraid to afraid to start at the bottom and work your way up. And so that's what I had intentions on and uh you know by the time I finished my career there I was in executive administration there for the pharmacy department and I was really really proud of what I was doing there. Um but I also realized that there was so much more that I could be doing with my voice. I realized that I was sitting there and I'm like man I'm sitting here helping these people build their company. I could be building my own dream and my own company. Um, and so I realized that I needed to just let my faith kick in and start allowing myself to trust um, you know, what God was trying to do in my life. And and the fact that, you know, my journey didn't necessarily look like everyone else is in my family. That was a little scary at first. Um, but I realized that, you know, helping families to protect their income and help them to eliminate their debt and and to really multiply their wealth. That was something that was really important to me. And so we decided to start the Max Life Agency. Uh and then from there we started helping others to retire themselves as well like we did. So [laughter] I love it. I love it. Yeah. Finding your purpose is so important to finding your place in the world. Once you find what your purpose, what God wants you to be doing, it's pretty easy then to, you know, when you have faith and you have vision, it's easy to make that next step into wherever God wants you to be. I feel like that's that you definitely stepped into that in a place of faith and I love that. >> Thank you. >> What was your first step in transforming your own financial story into a movement that now impacts so many others? My first step I'd say is just really really allowing myself to understand the importance of being true to me, you know, and really understanding how to build my own um foundation and understanding how to allow faith to really lead me to do that was something that was really really important to me. For me it was just um you know asking myself you know about the things that I had questions about and trying to understand okay if I have these questions and I have these issues and there's I know so many people are like me sitting in this situation if I can go and figure out the answers and be the solution. I really truly believe I heard Miles Monroe say one time uh that you know God has created you to be the answer to a question that is asked in a in your generation. And so I started looking for the question that was being asked that I could be the answer to. And uh I think that you start off by realizing that you have the question and then once you start answering it then you realize that you want to answer for more people. And so that was really the I think the stepping stones for me. I was really um you know I I was married before my first husband. Uh my first husband passed away. I found myself in a position of having uh you know children, being a single mom and then not knowing anything about finances. And so I realized there were so many people that would find themselves in that situation. And so as I dug myself out of it, I, you know, my husband and I, we decided to open, as we were, you know, friends and dating later on, we decided to open up our business together and to really help empower people in this area. So I would say that's that's how for me um, and just really teaching people the things that that they have everyday questions to. How do I retire? Where do I put my money? How do I grow my money? You know, how do I position myself to educate myself when there's so much information out there? are like what do I trust? Where do I go? Um and so we decided to do that and also to create um an ecosystem of people to give access to so that people have a easier opportunity to answer those questions for themselves. I love it. Finances are complex. It's you know I even I find myself going okay let's look at we're I found myself talking to Chat GBT this morning. What's the difference between a self-directed Roth IRA? what you know if I wanted to do have a a real an investment for real estate like vehicle I can do that with it was talking about like self-direct self-directed 401ks all these things like there's all these terms and it's like okay so who can you go to when you need those questions answered and you spring >> you guys can do that right >> absolutely absolutely >> because I feel like chat can take you so far and you can be okay now I have the right words but like how do you even do this You know, it's you can learn a lot from chat GBT, but then you really need people. >> Yes. Exactly. Exactly. And then it's drilling down to which people and who is the best for my situation because everybody has their own, you know, financial picture. And it's almost like having all of these puzzle pieces scattered all across the place and you're trying to look for your one little piece and how it fits into your puzzle to make your full financial picture. And so for us, we just are really passionate about helping people find their puzzle pieces and getting to the right people that has that piece for them and putting the whole thing together at once. I love that. So that is amazing. So you've really created an ecosystem that can help people with all all of the aspects of finance. I love that. You talk a lot about alignment. How did faith and purpose guide your decisions during the transition? For me, I feel like faith is really my foundation. Um, you know, I have always had trust. Um, that purpose is always going to pay more than position. And so, if you're in in a position without purpose, then you're going to be fruitless. And so, if you want to be fruitful, you have to understand like why am I here? What has God directed and planted my feet here? For what reason, right? And and God doesn't ever give vision without provision. And so I think that he really requires us to have that obedience first. And he's always going to require you to position yourself to do something that maybe is scary or that is um uncomfortable to step out and to walk in obedience. And so for me, every step of of that transition, it required prayer, it required patience, it required perspective. I decided really early on not to chase money, but to really just be chasing meaning and and you know, in that, you know, understanding that that was what I was doing. That's when things really started to multiply for us. And so, I'm super grateful that we've had that foundation and that clarity and that we understand like what our solid rock foundation is on. Yes. Love that. Yes. Totally. purpose over money all the time because if you're chasing dollars, I just feel like that's not it's never really seems like that's not how you make money. >> I've seen it my whole life. You know, I'm 46 years old and I've seen people chase dollars and it doesn't work. But when you follow purpose and you're in a place of passion and purpose and faith and you're doing things for the right reason, it just happens. That's when it's happened for me in my life. So I love that that mindset shift of you know finding the purpose first. The why >> the why am I doing this is so big. >> Yeah absolutely your why is going to connect you to everything. You know your why is going to be why you keep getting up why you keep pushing when things get hard why you sustain right and so if you don't have clarity on that then you probably are not going to be able have longevity. And so that's why it's super important to have that foundational understanding. I love it. I love it. That's so good. You're so wise, Spring. Love it. So, you've said that money is a tool for freedom, not just wealth. Could you unpack what that means to you? >> Yeah. So, I think that money it it really just is a multiplier of who you are, right? And so, um, it's really an understanding of the tool that it is and not having it be a distraction for you because I think some people they're chasing money, they have no idea why. You know, maybe it's just four things or whatever, but there's never going to be any fulfillment of that, right? And so I think that when you say like freedom, it's really important to understand that that's really the freedom of ability to make choices not based off off of survival but based off of vision. And so when you want to have financial freedom, it's because you're in a position to carry out a vision. And I believe that you are blessed according to the the the answers that you are solving. the like the bigger the vision, the more people that can fit into it, the more that your blessings create blessings for other people, that's the more that you're going to be blessed and given to. And so I think it's that true wealth is just really when money is working not just with you, but also for you. And so we want to always position people to understand like what is your vision, where are you going, and um and and help people to understand how they can turn that annual income into a monthly income stream. um so that we can help them to strategize to build out their legacy and to build out their vision and to build out whatever it is that they're called to do. Um because freedom is not just about having everything. It's about having options and then when you have those options, knowing what to do with them. So that's a really important step. But I think that that we sometimes skew that freedom and what financial freedom is for. It's not for me to be able to eat crab legs whenever I want. Although that's also a awesome, [laughter] you know, it's about, you know, the freedom to change lives and the freedom to do things that are going to leave impact. Absolutely. And that's what it's been about for me. I honestly have to say, finding a place where I found my purpose and now I'm in a place where I've found that the impact part of my life, where I'm like, now I'm in the legacy and impact part of my life. And there's something really beautiful about being in this place now and doing things because I want to do them for society. I want to make the world a better place. I want to shine a light on women. I want to help women rise and elevate and you know find a place of equality for all of us. And for the women listening who might still feel disempowered by money or intimidated by financial planning, where can they start? >> I think awareness. You know, I think that for women a lot of times we are so wrapped up in the area of finance in shame um that we're not able to get to awareness. Um and so I think there's that block that we have to understand, but we also have to know that the obstacle is the way, right? Shout out Ryan Holiday. It's a great book. But [laughter] >> you know, um >> we'll put that in the show notes. We'll link that book. [laughter] That's >> right. You know, but you have to know that that like having awareness that if you have an obstacle there, don't have shame around it. Go get educated, right? Sometimes when you look at numbers, they tell a story, right? And so you have to surround yourself with people who speak wealth fluently and you have to understand like, okay, what is my story telling me? Where is the direction of my life going? And it like my story is not just going to drastically change without there being a plot twist, right? And so the plot twist is having the right people that can help you. You don't need to know everything. You just need to be connected to the right people and that might have the right systems and mentorship that can help you get where you're trying to go. And so that's exactly why we built, you know, the Max Life Agency. That's why we built the Legacy Lab so that we can help people to find a safe space and a place where they can learn, where they can ask questions, um, where they can grow their confidence and where they have a lot of clarity. And so I believe confidence increases when clarity is there. And so that's the most important thing. Get clarity. Don't be afraid to ask questions. And don't feel like you have to know everything. I know some of the most wealthy people that I know, they have the most questions. And so it's not like some people are afraid to ask because they feel like they're supposed to already know. And that's not a reality, right? [laughter] So it's like don't be afraid to ask no matter where you are. If you're in a position of you've already gotten yourself in a ton of debt and you're trying to figure it out or you have a ton of money and you feel ashamed that you don't know what to do with it, whatever the case, just stop walking in shame and start walking in awareness. >> I love it. I love it. Spring. Yeah, education is everything. And then being surrounded. I always said I don't know something. I'm going to hire somebody who does know that thing. You know, that's that's been the secret to my success is, you know, I don't need to know everything. I don't need to know how to do everything. I just need to know somebody who can do that thing or knows that thing that I don't know how to do. >> Exactly. [laughter] >> I'm not going to reinvent the wheel. So, so financial planners, we need to go talk to financial planners. Someone like you is a financial planner. Is that the word if we're going to teach the girls a word? [laughter] >> Yes. But, you know, I think that like I was talking about those puzzle pieces. There's a there's a lot of different pieces to that puzzle. You might have a financial planner. You might have an insurance broker. You might have, you know, somebody that's helping you with investments. You might have a real estate broker. You know, there's different people that work within that puzzle. And that's why I like our ecosystem that we created with Legacy Lab. It creates a space for all of those different people. Um, because that's the thing that's hard is that everybody that's is an expert in their area. So, they only speak the language of their piece. And so our thing is like trying to give you a palatable entry way into understanding and having introductions into relationships of everywhere uh of everybody that you might need to know. But yeah, I think that there's a lot of things that get left off of the table when you talk about finances. People already they always think, oh, financial planner, oh, they think I need to put my money into investments or I need to do a saving or a 401k or something like that. But a lot of times um insurance products, annuities, that kind of stuff gets left off of the table and it's a really important piece to your financial picture as well. Um or you're thinking of retirement or whatever or you know, real estate, you know, there's just so many pieces that sometimes get left off of that picture. So just talk to people. I'm happy to be that person. I'll fall on the sword for you. No, [laughter] >> but you know, just someone that you trust um that you can reach out to to start asking questions um and just get those answers. That's the most important thing. Take a step at a time. That's the biggest thing. You can't eat an elephant in a day. You have to take it a bite at a time. So, that's the biggest thing. I love it. I love it. I think so many women feel I do think we feel imp, you know, we do feel ashamed because we really haven't had this kind of education. We haven't stood around on the golf courses and talked about our financial planners like the guys do. And I really want to open that world up to the to women and say, "Hey, let's have these kinds of conversations. If we know somebody awesome like Spring, let's, you know, share that information. Let's make sure we have the resources to improve and elevate ourselves because if we make money and we don't have good tax strategies or we don't have good investment strategies or we don't have good insurance, any of that stuff can take us down. And you know, it's sad, but I was just hearing a statistic that the largest growing population of homeless people are are senior-aged women. >> Wow. That's really really offputting. That's so sad. especially because it can really be prevented. But you're right, a lot of times financial literacy, it feels like it's a man's world, you know, and so we have to show up for each other and we have and that's why I love that you have this this podcast because we have to show up for each other and learn how to manage her manage our finances and our things and the things that are important to us. And you know, it's crazy because um with that being one statistic, the other statistic is understanding that women outlive men, right? And so uh a lot of times women are actually the largest inheritors of wealth. Um but unfortunately because the financial planner and the person that that their husband, you know, set up with, they weren't sitting in those meetings, they don't necessarily trust those people. their husbands might have because men are typically more aggressive when it comes to the way that they want to grow or spend their money. Women are a lot more conservative. Um, and so sometimes they don't know that they trust that person or they don't want to continue doing things the same way that their husbands did. And so that's that's where we have to start as well is like educate yourself before you need to understand these things because there's a lot like you would be surprised how many housewives there are in the world or how many women that are even partners in business with their spouse but they just have no idea about the financial side. And so that is the biggest piece that we have to close the gap for is helping you understand different products, different vehicles, different things that you can utilize where you can still grow your money but you can do it in a conservative way. You don't have to ri have risk. You don't have to position yourself to lose any money. But people think when investment or uh growing their money comes into play that that means automatically I have to take on some risk. But that's just not the case. It's just the only thing that we've really been taught about and that's what I'm really passionate about teaching people about is conservative ways that they can grow their money with safety and making sure that they are never going to position themselves to lose principle. And so I of course I'm a woman. That's something that I'm passionate about is making sure that we have those sound things that we are positioning ourselves and our money into as well. So I love that. I love that so much. And what are some of the most common [snorts] limiting beliefs that you see women carrying around money and how do we rewrite those? >> I think that we are kind of like it's not said but it's there's kind of like a unknown of like women you shouldn't be talking about finances or money or you know you're doing too much if you're trying to get involved. You know, Proverbs says that wisdom is what builds a house, right? And so, we want to make sure that we are building a financial house and we're building it on the right foundation. So, we have to start talking about it. We ha we have to understand stewardship and understanding stewardship is not just spiritual. It's also in our finances. It's the way that we live our life. It's the way that we manage ourselves. And so, the belief that money is evil is I think also an issue that we have as women. um or that somehow success means selfishness or or something like that. You know, that that's something that we have to kind of get past and understand that success is important and it's what God wants for us. He wants us to have abundance so that we can do more for other people. Um the other thing I think that is a limiting belief for women is that um there's just an understanding that oh we are all frivolous. Oh, we just shop too much or oh we just you know do all of these things and we don't have to subscribe to that. Like I have this saying that I always say to people is like only fools pay full price, right? And it's like you can have nice things. You can do things, you know, in a certain way and live a a certain type of life, but do it in a budget and a a moneys smart way. Do it in a way that makes sense for you. I challenge uh women to rewrite that and realize that that wealth is really just it's the important thing when it's in the right hands, right? And that's what heals our community. And most of us have a big heart for the community. Most women are compassionate and loving and want to change things for people. But if you don't have the finances, if you don't have the means, then you can't do much, right? And so it's it's just a magnifier. Like I said earlier, it re it just reveals who you already are. And so if you are a good-hearted person, if you're somebody that you want to change things in your life, in your world, or in the world of others, then you have to get on the track of understanding. And so I think just taking that limiting belief off of like I can't do this or you know I've never done it before. No one in my family's ever been wealthy so that means I can't. You have to take those limiting beliefs off and just let yourself take the like be ruthless, right? Not ruthless but ruthless. [laughter] Oh, I like it. I love it. I know. And I feel like there's a bossiness, you know, this like, oh, if you get involved with the finances, you're being bossy. And I feel like that need, I know, within the relationship.
